# Definition of Ready Checklist

An issue must satisfy **all** of the following criteria before it can be pulled into a sprint or iteration. This prevents ambiguous, unbounded, or unworkable items from entering active development.

---

## Checklist

### Problem & Value
- [ ] The problem statement or user need is clearly described.
- [ ] The business or customer value is articulated (why does this matter?).
- [ ] Success criteria or measurable outcome are defined.

### Scope & Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Acceptance criteria are written in a testable format (e.g., Given / When / Then or explicit conditions).
- [ ] Scope is understood and bounded — it is clear what is *in* and *out* of scope.
- [ ] Edge cases and error scenarios have been considered.

### Dependencies & Risks
- [ ] External dependencies are identified and unblocked (or a mitigation is in place).
- [ ] Technical risks are noted in the issue or the risk register.

### Ownership & Estimation
- [ ] A single owner (assignee) has been identified.
- [ ] The item has been estimated or sized (e.g., story points, T-shirt size).
- [ ] The item is small enough to complete within one sprint/iteration; if not, it has been split.

### Supporting Information
- [ ] Relevant designs, specs, or reference links are attached or linked.
- [ ] The issue is linked to any parent epic or milestone.

---

## When to use this checklist
- During backlog refinement to assess readiness.
- At sprint/iteration planning before pulling an item into the sprint.
- When reviewing issues raised via the issue template.

---

## Tips
- If an item fails multiple criteria, assign it back to the author or BA with comments.
- "Definition of Ready" is a team agreement — review and update this checklist in retrospectives if criteria are too strict or too loose.

---

*Part of the [OctoAcme Ways of Working](../ways-of-working.md). See also: [Definition of Done](definition-of-done.md).*
